definitions: A phrase used to describe the aftermath of an explosion, fire, or other destructive event, characterized by the presence of airborne particulate matter and gaseous byproducts.
A general term for the chaotic and unclear conditions following a significant disturbance.
examples: After the building collapsed, there was nothing but dust and smoke.
The battle left behind a scene of dust and smoke.
Through the dust and smoke, they could barely make out the outline of the structure.
